title: Add num_locks to the default libpod config
body:
Allow adjusting number of locks in libpod.conf via an already available knob we previously didn't expose in the default config file.

Sort of blocked by #2350 given the references to the unmerged renumber command

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I think the num_locks in libpod.conf needs to sit above the [runtimes] section. I wound up putting it right under the commented-out label block. It's possible I'm just behind master by a few days and need to re-pull, so disregard this if this has already been addressed.

@mheon I re-pulled and still see this issue when num_locks is at the bottom:
```
ERRO[0000] could not get runtime: error decoding configuration from file libpod.conf: Type mismatch for 'libpod.RuntimeConfig.runtimes': Expected slice but found 'int64'.
```
Does a `bin/podman --config libpod.conf info` work for you on that file?

I think this might be broken with `--config` but I can confirm that modifying the default `libpod.conf` does work.

I think we might need to do a more comprehensive rework of `--config` (and `NewRuntimeFromConfig`) to fix them

I see, maybe youre using this for a diff use case than me. Not necessarily opposed to deferring those `--config` fixes for a different PR, but fwiw if you put the num_locks config variable above the [runtimes] section it works without changes in that scenario too.

...Oh dear.

Alright, that's properly strange. I don't... I don't like that this is a thing that happens. But since CI is failing again, I'll make the changes and repush.
